meta.section: Endpoint Management — Windows (Phase 7 of 7: Apps + enrollment / provisioning)
meta.source: Microsoft Learn (learn.microsoft.com/intune + autopilot + windows/configuration) — portal paths, Autopilot/ESP settings, Store policies grounded via the Microsoft Learn MCP on 2026-07-03.
generalNotes: This phase mixes three things: Settings Catalog config profiles (Store control, remove built-in apps), an app deployment (Company Portal, under Apps), and two Windows Enrollment profiles (Autopilot deployment profile + Enrollment Status Page).

The provisioning trio works together for new/reset devices: the Autopilot profile shapes the OOBE, the ESP blocks the desktop until setup completes, and Company Portal + your config/security profiles install during that window. Set them up together and test with one pilot device before onboarding staff.
Existing, already-configured devices are unaffected by the Autopilot/ESP profiles — those only shape the out-of-box experience.

requiredSettings.label: Profile type
requiredSettings.value: Settings catalog — Windows Components > Store (user scope)

requiredSettings.label: Intent
requiredSettings.value: Limit consumer Store installs while keeping IT-approved/private-store apps available per baseline

steps: Devices > Configuration > Create > Windows 10 and later > Settings catalog.
Add the user-scoped Store settings (restrict private store / limit consumer installs) per the baseline; assign to the Windows device group.
Note: LOW impact — IT-approved apps stay available; personal Store installs are limited. Pairs with the device-scoped Store profile below.

requiredSettings.label: Profile type
requiredSettings.value: Settings catalog — Windows Components > Store (device scope)

requiredSettings.label: Key setting
requiredSettings.value: 'Turn off the Store application' (per baseline) — NOTE: this still lets Intune deploy Store apps and lets winget work; UWP auto-updates continue unless you also disable them

steps: Devices > Configuration > Create > Windows 10 and later > Settings catalog.

Add the device-scoped Store control ('Turn off the Store application' is preferred over 'private store only'); leave 'Turn off automatic download/install of updates' Not configured so built-in/UWP apps still auto-update.
Assign to the Windows device group.
Note: LOW impact — blocks end-user consumer Store access without breaking Intune app delivery or winget.

requiredSettings.label: Profile type
requiredSettings.value: Settings catalog — Administrative Templates > Windows Components > App Package Deployment

requiredSettings.label: Remove default Microsoft Store packages from the system
requiredSettings.value: Enabled; toggle each consumer app to remove (Xbox, Candy Crush, Clipchamp, etc.)

steps: Devices > Configuration > Create > Windows 10 and later > Settings catalog.
Add 'Remove default Microsoft Store packages from the system' = Enabled and set each unwanted app's toggle to True; assign to a Windows DEVICE group.

Note (MEDIUM impact): removals happen at OOBE / new-profile provisioning / sign-in after the policy applies — for a seamless result apply during Autopilot with the ESP blocking. Removing an app also removes its local data, and you can't re-add it by un-toggling (requires reprovisioning) — so notify users and choose deliberately.

requiredSettings.label: Where
requiredSettings.value: Apps > Windows > Add > Microsoft Store app (new) — Company Portal

steps: Intune admin center > Apps > Windows > Add > app type 'Microsoft Store app (new)', search Company Portal, select it.
Assign as Required to the Windows device group with install behavior = System (machine-wide).
It installs silently via Windows Package Manager and appears in Start; on first launch users sign in with their work account.
Note: LOW impact — gives users a self-service surface for approved apps + compliance status; deploy it as part of provisioning.

requiredSettings.label: Where
requiredSettings.value: Devices > Windows > Windows enrollment > Deployment Profiles > Create

requiredSettings.label: Deployment mode
requiredSettings.value: User-driven; Join to Microsoft Entra ID as = Microsoft Entra joined

requiredSettings.label: OOBE
requiredSettings.value: Hide EULA (Microsoft Software License Terms), Hide Privacy settings, Hide change account options, Automatically configure keyboard = Yes (skip keyboard page)

requiredSettings.label: User account type
requiredSettings.value: Standard (per baseline — pairs with the Local Admins lockdown in Phase 3)

steps: Devices > Windows > Windows enrollment > Deployment Profiles > Create profile > Windows PC.
OOBE: Deployment mode = User-driven; Entra joined; hide EULA + privacy + change-account options; skip keyboard; set User account type = Standard.
Assign to the Autopilot devices group (optionally 'Convert all targeted devices to Autopilot' = Yes).
Note (MEDIUM impact): shapes the out-of-box setup for new/reset devices into a streamlined, branded experience — no effect on existing configured devices.

requiredSettings.label: Where
requiredSettings.value: Devices > Windows > Windows enrollment > Enrollment Status Page (Default profile or a new one)

requiredSettings.label: Show app and profile installation progress
Block device use until all apps and profiles are installed

steps: Devices > Windows > Windows enrollment > Enrollment Status Page > open the Default profile (assigned to All users and all devices) or create one.
Set Show installation progress = Yes and Block device use until all apps and profiles are installed = Yes; tune error-handling (reset/allow-use on failure) per baseline.

Note (MEDIUM impact): during onboarding the new device is held on the ESP until setup finishes — first sign-in can take 15–45+ minutes depending on apps/policies. Prepare new users for the wait; existing devices are unaffected.
